title: Xtra Dense Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A buttery, dense, and comforting Moroccan dish featuring lamb, muscovado sugar, and a blend of spices, cooked to perfection in a tagine using a bain-marie method.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the lamb pieces, muscovado sugar, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, and mix well.
In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat, then add the lamb mixture and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent.
Transfer the lamb mixture to a tagine or a large ceramic dish with a tight-fitting lid.
Add the chicken broth and dried prunes to the tagine, then cover with the lid.
Place the tagine in a larger dish or pan and add enough hot water to come halfway up the sides of the tagine.
Cover the larger dish or pan with aluminum foil and bake for 2.5 hours.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes.
Remove the tagine from the oven and stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter until melted.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
